title: Getting started with axR
text: axR talks Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometer devices: discovery, status, settings, data download, – recent addition – reading recorded .cwa/AX6 binary files directly. wrapping Open Movement Project’s OMAPI C library (vendored src/omapi), rather reimplementing serial protocol binary file format scratch. OMAPI library behind Axivity’s OmGui software. ’s axivity_open()/close() step – OMAPI session starts automatically axR loaded, every device-facing function takes device_id get axivity_discover().

title: Getting started with axR
text: returns one row per connected device, device_id, serial, port, path, firmware_version, hardware_version, battery_level. Everything else axR talks live device takes device_id table.

title: Getting started with axR
text: handful read-queries – safe run time: change device state, harmless reversible: axivity_set_led() accepts "auto", "", "blue", "green", "cyan", "red", "magenta", "yellow", "white".
code: axivity_get_battery(id) axivity_self_test(id) axivity_get_memory_health(id) axivity_get_accelerometer(id)   # instantaneous x/y/z, in g axivity_get_time(id)            # device's RTC, as a POSIXct axivity_set_time(id, Sys.time())   # sync the device clock axivity_set_led(id, "blue")        # visible, easy way to confirm the                                     # write path is working

title: Getting started with axR
text: Delayed activation windows, session IDs, metadata, accelerometer configuration can read set: axivity_get_delays()/axivity_set_delays() use -Inf/Inf R-side sentinels OMAPI’s “always”/“never” delay values.
code: axivity_get_delays(id) axivity_get_session_id(id) axivity_get_metadata(id) axivity_get_accel_config(id)  axivity_set_accel_config(id, rate = 100, range = 8)  # 100 Hz, +/-8g

title: Getting started with axR
text: level one "none" (commit metadata – recommended, can cause data/metadata mismatch), "delete", "quickformat" (default), "wipe" (slowest, thorough). Staged settings changes (delays, session ID, metadata, accelerometer config) take full effect called – see OMAPI’s documentation detail . erases device. ’s undo.

title: Getting started with axR
text: long downloads, blocking = FALSE lets poll instead waiting:
code: info <- axivity_get_data_info(id) info  # size, filename, block layout, and the recorded time range  axivity_download(id, "session.cwa")  # blocks until done by default axivity_download(id, "session.cwa", blocking = FALSE) axivity_download_status(id)   # check progress without blocking axivity_download_wait(id)     # block until it finishes, whenever you're ready # axivity_download_cancel(id) # if you change your mind

what: If discovery isn’t finding your device
title: Getting started with axR
text: OMAPI’s device discovery device’s USB mass-storage mount two independent things – ’s possible storage side mount appear Finder/Explorer just fine OMAPI’s IOKit/SetupAPI device matching doesn’t find (happened axR’s development – see NEWS.md). happens, can see device’s volume directly: bypasses OMAPI/device_id entirely – ’s plain file copy whatever path give .
code: list.files("/Volumes")  # macOS -- find the device's volume name  axivity_copy_data("/Volumes/AX317_46171", "~/axr_data")

title: Getting started with axR
text: axivity_read_cwa() doesn’t need live device – works .cwa/AX6 file already disk, whether ’s one downloaded axivity_download()/axivity_copy_data(), file sitting directly device’s still-mounted volume (’s need copy first just read ): One row per sample: timestamp (POSIXct, sub-second precision), x/y/z (accelerometer, g), gx/gy/gz mx//mz (gyroscope/magnetometer, present recording – e.g. AX6 GA/GAM mode), light/temperature_c/battery_pct/ sample_rate (last four read per data block repeated across block’s samples, truly per-sample readings). device_id session_id handy quick sanity check ’ve opened file think . device_id independently cross-checked ioreg Axivity config web tool physical device. Known caveat: temperature_c currently trusted. vendored conversion formula specific one temperature sensor chip, ’s note beside source alternate formula different hardware – see ?axivity_read_cwa NEWS.md detail. Everything else output (timestamps, x/y/z, device_id) verified real device.

title: Getting started with axR
text: tibble axivity_read_cwa() returns meant clean starting point downstream wrist-actigraphy analysis zeitR – one row per sample, standard column names, POSIXct timestamps, accelerometer values already g rather device-raw units. (zeitR’s actual entry point may differ – check zeitR’s documentation current expected input shape.)

title: Getting started with axR
text: axR originally scoped “dumb pipe”: talk device, move bytes, leave file parsing downstream packages. axivity_read_cwa() deliberate exception – OMAPI already ships complete .cwa reader, wrapping directly avoids building second, differently-sourced parser format. axR still doesn’t higher-level actigraphy analysis (sleep detection, non-wear detection, etc.) data reads – ’s zeitR’s job, downstream tibble returns.

title: Getting started with axR
text: See NEWS.md full, current list, briefly: Device discovery real issues least one tested machine (macOS 26.2) despite device enumerating correctly USB/IOKit level – root cause still investigated. axivity_copy_data() workaround meantime. Windows device discovery uses fixed COM1-COM40 probe range rather true enumeration. temperature_c axivity_read_cwa() unverified likely wrong least hardware revisions. None tested AX6 (real AX3).

what: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
title: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
text: Device discovery, status, settings, data download Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometer devices.

title: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
text: axR talks Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometers USB: discovering connected devices, querying setting status/configuration, downloading recorded .cwa files. Rather reimplementing Axivity serial protocol directly, axR wraps Open Movement Project’s OMAPI C library (vendored src/omapi, BSD 2-clause, Newcastle University — see src/omapi/LICENSE.TXT). OMAPI library behind Axivity’s OmGui software, includes maintained, platform-specific device discovery (IOKit/DiskArbitration macOS, SetupAPI Windows, udev Linux) rather hand-rolled equivalent. axR deliberately “dumb pipe” — doesn’t know anything .cwa file structure. Parsing recorded data left downstream packages mrpheus zeitR. Status: implemented OMAPI yet tested physical AX3/AX6 device.

title: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
text: 🔍 Discovery — axivity_discover() 📊 Status — battery, self-test, memory health, live accelerometer reading, RTC get/set, LED colour, anti-tamper lock, ECC flag, raw axivity_send_command() escape hatch ⚙️ Settings — delayed activation window, session ID, metadata scratch buffer, accelerometer rate/range, axivity_reset() (erase + commit, none/delete/quickformat/wipe levels) 📥 Download — axivity_download(), backed OMAPI’s background download thread (progress polling cancellation included), plain file copy ’s axivity_open()/close() step — OMAPI session starts axR loaded stops ’s unloaded. Every function takes device_id axivity_discover().

title: axR — Device communication and .cwa file reading for Axivity devices
code: axR/ ├── configure              # generates src/Makevars at install time (chmod +x!) ├── R/ │   ├── axR-package.R   # package-level documentation │   ├── zzz.R            # .onLoad/.onUnload (OmStartup/OmShutdown), .om_check() │   ├── discover.R        # axivity_discover() │   ├── status.R          # battery, self-test, memory health, accelerometer, │   │                       # RTC, LED, lock, ECC, send_command │   ├── settings.R        # delays, session ID, metadata, accel config, reset │   └── download.R        # data info, download, download_status/wait/cancel ├── src/ │   ├── axR-omapi.cpp     # thin Rcpp wrapper around OMAPI │   ├── omapi/             # vendored OMAPI C library (BSD 2-clause) │   ├── Makevars.in       # template; configure fills in the platform-specific bits │   └── Makevars.win      # Windows build config (static, no template needed) ├── tests/testthat/ ├── man/figures/logo.svg ├── DESCRIPTION └── NEWS.md

what: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
title: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
text: Talks Axivity AX3/AX6 accelerometer devices: discovery, status (battery, self-test, memory health, accelerometer, RTC, LED, lock, ECC), settings (delays, session ID, metadata, accelerometer config, erase), data download, reading recorded .cwa/AX6 binary files (axivity_read_cwa()).

title: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
text: axR originally scoped "dumb pipe" – talk device, move bytes, leave file parsing downstream packages. axivity_read_cwa() deliberate exception: OMAPI already ships complete binary file reader (omapi-reader.c), wrapping directly simpler consistent reimplementing format second time zeitR different reference pipeline. axR higher-level actigraphy analysis parsed data (sleep detection, non-wear detection, etc.) – still zeitR's job, downstream tibble returns.

title: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
text: Rather reimplementing Axivity serial protocol .cwa binary format directly, axR wraps Open Movement Project's OMAPI C library (vendored src/omapi, BSD 2-clause, Newcastle University – see src/omapi/LICENSE.TXT). OMAPI library behind Axivity's OmGui software, includes maintained, platform-specific device discovery (IOKit/DiskArbitration macOS, SetupAPI Windows, udev Linux) rather hand-rolled equivalent. OMAPI session started axR loaded (OmStartup() .onLoad()) shut unloaded (OmShutdown() .onUnload()) – separate axivity_open()/close() step. Every device-facing function takes device_id, obtained axivity_discover(). axivity_read_cwa() axivity_copy_data() exceptions – work file already disk need live device connection .

title: axR: Device Communication and .cwa File Reading for Axivity Devices — axR-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Mario Leocadio-Miguel mario.miguel@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

what: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
title: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
text: fallback axivity_discover()/OMAPI device access working, device's USB mass-storage volume mounts visible Finder/diskutil regardless – common split, since storage side OMAPI's IOKit-level device discovery independent paths (see NEWS.md discovery issues hit far). bypasses OMAPI device_id entirely: plain file copy, nothing . axR parse .cwa contents – see mrpheus zeitR .

title: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
code: axivity_copy_data(   device_path,   dest_dir,   pattern = "\\.cwa$",   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
text: device_path Character. Path mounted device volume, e.g. "/Volumes/CWA17_46171". Find Finder, list.files("/Volumes"). dest_dir Character. Destination directory downloaded files. Created (recursively) already exist. pattern Character. Regex filter files copied, matched case-insensitively. Default "\\.cwa$". overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing files destination. Default FALSE.

title: Copy recorded data directly off a mounted Axivity volume — axivity_copy_data
text: Character vector destination file paths successfully copied.

title: Discover connected Axivity devices — axivity_discover
text: Wraps OMAPI's OmGetDeviceIds() plus handful per-device status calls single data frame. Unlike serial-port probing, uses OMAPI's device discovery – including platform-specific finder (IOKit/DiskArbitration macOS, SetupAPI Windows, udev Linux) – behave way OmGui machine.

title: Discover connected Axivity devices — axivity_discover
Discover connected Axivity devices — axivity_discover
text: data frame one row per connected device: device_id, serial, port, path, firmware_version, hardware_version, battery_level. Zero rows devices connected.

what: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
title: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
text: Wraps OMAPI's OmBeginDownloading(), runs download background thread inside library. axR parse .cwa contents – see mrpheus zeitR .

title: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
code: axivity_download(   device_id,   dest_file,   offset_blocks = 0L,   length_blocks = -1L,   blocking = TRUE )

title: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover(). dest_file Character. Destination file path (existing files path truncated). offset_blocks Integer. Start offset download, blocks. Default 0. length_blocks Integer. Length download, blocks. Default -1 (). blocking Logical. TRUE (default), block download completes, fails, cancelled – equivalent calling axivity_download_wait() immediately . FALSE, return soon download starts; poll axivity_download_status().

title: Download recorded data off an Axivity device — axivity_download
text: blocking = TRUE, list status (one "complete", "error", "cancelled") value (diagnostic code status "error"). blocking = FALSE, invisibly NULL.

title: Enable OMAPI's internal debug log — axivity_enable_debug_log
text: Important: controls log lines go. Whether anything logged controlled OMAPI's debug level, read OMDEBUG environment variable , OmStartup() time – .e. library(axR) runs. seeing log output calling , set OMDEBUG (e.g. Sys.setenv(OMDEBUG = "3")) loading axR, fresh R session, call function retry.

what: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
title: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
text: Uses OMAPI's binary file reader (omapi-reader.c, vendored libomapi) parse .cwa/AX6 recording directly, rather reimplementing binary format scratch. Returns one row per sample – ready hand zeitR, downstream actigraphy analysis.

title: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
text: path Character. Path .cwa/AX6 file.

title: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
text: tibble (plain data frame, tibble package installed) one row per sample: timestamp POSIXct, UTC, sub-second precision x, y, z Accelerometer readings, g gx, gy, gz Gyroscope readings, raw units (present recording gyroscope, e.g. AX6 GA/GAM mode) mx, , mz Magnetometer readings, raw units (present recording magnetometer, e.g. AX6 GAM mode) light Raw light sensor reading temperature_c Temperature degrees Celsius. Unverified, possibly wrong – OMAPI's conversion (OM_VALUE_TEMPERATURE_MC) hardcodes formula one specific temperature sensor chip (MCP9700); comment beside vendored source notes alternate formula different chip (MCP9701), suggesting may hardware/revision-specific. Cross-check OmGui's reading file relying . battery_pct Battery percentage, time block sample_rate Sampling rate Hz, time block device_id, session_id, metadata attached attributes. timestamp, x/y/z, device_id verified correct real AX3 file (cross-checked device_id ioreg Axivity config web tool); temperature_c .

title: Read a .cwa/AX6 binary file into a tibble — axivity_read_cwa
text: Unlike rest axR, function talk live device – works file already disk (e.g. one retrieved axivity_copy_data() axivity_download()), require axivity_discover() found anything.

what: Erase an Axivity device's data storage and commit settings — axivity_reset
title: Erase an Axivity device's data storage and commit settings — axivity_reset
text: Wraps OMAPI's OmEraseDataAndCommit(). Staged settings changes (delays, session ID, metadata, accelerometer config) take full effect called.

title: Erase an Axivity device's data storage and commit settings — axivity_reset
Erase an Axivity device's data storage and commit settings — axivity_reset
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover(). level One "none" (commit metadata , recommended – can cause data/metadata mismatch), "delete" (remove recreate data file), "quickformat" (recreate filesystem), "wipe" (clear NAND blocks, cleanest slowest).

Send a raw command to an Axivity device — axivity_send_command
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover(). command Character. command string send. expected Character. expected response prefix, "" specified. timeout_ms Integer. Timeout milliseconds. Default 2000.

title: Set an Axivity device's LED colour — axivity_set_led
Set an Axivity device's LED colour — axivity_set_led
text: device_id Integer device ID, returned axivity_discover(). colour One "auto" (default device-controlled behaviour), "", "blue", "green", "cyan", "red", "magenta", "yellow", "white".

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial scaffold: DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE, Rcpp/C++ src layout.

what: Architecture change: wraps OMAPI rather than reimplementing the serial protocol
title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: Retired hand-rolled termios.h/kernel32 serial implementation (moved attic/serial.cpp – part build). Vendored Open Movement Project’s OMAPI C library src/omapi (BSD 2-clause, Newcastle University; see src/omapi/LICENSE.TXT). OMAPI library behind Axivity’s OmGui, maintained platform-specific device discovery (IOKit/DiskArbitration macOS, SetupAPI Windows, udev Linux). src/axR-omapi.cpp: thin Rcpp wrapper – translates R types OMAPI’s int deviceId / -parameter / negative-error-code convention. device logic reimplemented . .onLoad()/.onUnload() (R/zzz.R) call OmStartup()/OmShutdown(); ’s separate axivity_open()/close() step. SystemRequirements DESCRIPTION: Linux needs libudev-dev (equivalent) device discovery. macOS/Windows use OS frameworks/APIs separate install step.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: axivity_discover(): now backed OmGetDeviceIds() plus per-device status calls, returning device_id, serial, port, path, firmware_version, hardware_version, battery_level.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: axivity_get_battery(), axivity_self_test(), axivity_get_memory_health(), axivity_get_accelerometer(), axivity_get_time()/set_time() (POSIXct), axivity_set_led(), axivity_is_locked()/set_lock()/unlock(), axivity_get_ecc()/ set_ecc(), axivity_send_command() (now backed OmCommand() instead raw serial).

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: axivity_get_delays()/set_delays() (-Inf/Inf R-side sentinels OMAPI’s zero/infinite OM_DATETIME values), axivity_get_session_id()/ set_session_id(), axivity_get_metadata()/set_metadata(), axivity_get_accel_config()/set_accel_config(). axivity_reset(): breaking change previous full/commit logical arguments – now takes level = c("none","delete","quickformat","wipe"), matching OMAPI’s OM_ERASE_LEVEL enum directly.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: axivity_download(): breaking change previous plain file.copy() version – now wraps OmBeginDownloading(), OMAPI’s background-thread download, axivity_download_status()/_wait()/ _cancel() polling cancellation. blocking = TRUE (default) waits completion; blocking = FALSE returns immediately. axivity_get_data_info(): file size, filename, block layout, recorded time range, OmGetDataFileSize()/OmGetDataFilename()/ OmGetDataRange(). axivity_copy_data(device_path, dest_dir, ...): fallback bypasses OMAPI/device_id entirely copies .cwa files straight mounted volume path – use axivity_discover() isn’t finding device via OMAPI’s IOKit-level discovery, mass-storage side still mounts fine regardless (testing – see “Known gaps” ). plain-file-copy logic original pre-OMAPI axivity_download() , just name doesn’t collide current OMAPI-backed one.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: Scope change “dumb pipe”: axR now wraps OMAPI’s binary file reader (omapi-reader.c, vendored, already compiled ) rather leaving .cwa/AX6 parsing zeitR. Chosen deliberately alternative (parity-first port Julia’s Python Condor pipeline zeitR) since OMAPI already ships complete, working reader – wrapping directly avoids second, differently-sourced parsing implementation. axR still doesn’t higher-level actigraphy analysis result (sleep detection, non-wear detection, etc.) – ’s still zeitR’s job downstream. axivity_read_cwa(path): whole block-reading loop runs C++, R (multi-day 100Hz recording millions samples; looping .Call()s per-block R real performance problem). Returns tibble (plain data frame tibble isn’t installed) one row per sample: timestamp (POSIXct, sub-second precision via OMAPI’s fractional timestamp), x/y/z (accelerometer, g), gx/gy/gz mx//mz (gyro/magnetometer, present recording – e.g. AX6 GA/GAM mode), plus light/temperature_c/battery_pct/sample_rate replicated block granularity (per-block readings, per-sample). device_id, session_id, metadata attached attributes. Doesn’t require [axivity_discover()] found anything – works .cwa/AX6 file already disk. Added tibble Suggests. Known issue, unverified/likely wrong: temperature_c came back -28.3 real AX3 (hardware rev 1.7) recording, isn’t plausible reading. vendored conversion omapi-reader.c (OM_VALUE_TEMPERATURE_MC) hardcodes formula one specific temperature sensor chip (MCP9700), comment right next noting alternate formula different chip (MCP9701) – suggesting conversion hardware/sensor-revision-specific may match every AX3 build. x/y/z, timestamps, device_id independently verified correct file; temperature_c verified trusted checked OmGui’s reading recording (Axivity’s hardware documentation sensor chip hardware revision uses).

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: C-level callbacks (OmSetDownloadCallback()/OmSetDeviceCallback()) – fire OMAPI’s background thread, calling back R non-R thread isn’t safe. Polling (axivity_download_status(), OmWaitForDownload() via axivity_download_wait()) used instead.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: .om_check() behaviour (list scalar status, pass-vs. error). axivity_discover() shape check device connected. axivity_reset()/axivity_set_led() argument validation (match.arg() failures), don’t require hardware. Prior serial-/O file-copy tests moved attic/ (tested now-retired API).

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: Added vignette (vignettes/axR.Rmd, vignette("axR")) walking discovery, status/settings, downloading, axivity_copy_data(), axivity_read_cwa(). VignetteBuilder: knitr added DESCRIPTION. axivity_read_cwa() section currently eval = TRUE .orig source, since ’s part axR verified working real hardware right now. Discovery/status/settings/ download chunks stay eval = FALSE (illustrative ) discovery fixed can genuinely re-run re-baked. Re-run knitr::knit() step re-commit axR.Rmd whenever verified-working parts package change, discovery starts working sections can flipped eval = TRUE.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: Added vignette (vignettes/axR.Rmd, vignette("axR")) walking discovery, status/settings, downloading, axivity_copy_data(), axivity_read_cwa(). VignetteBuilder: knitr added DESCRIPTION. axivity_read_cwa() section currently eval = TRUE .orig source, since ’s part axR verified working real hardware right now. Discovery/status/settings/ download chunks stay eval = FALSE (illustrative ) discovery fixed can genuinely re-run re-baked. Re-run knitr::knit() step re-commit axR.Rmd whenever verified-working parts package change, discovery starts working sections can flipped eval = TRUE.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: _pkgdown.yml: structure zeitR/mrpheus (Bootstrap 5 + bslib theming, OpenGraph meta, navbar/footer layout), using axR’s hex sticker palette (navy #014370, coral #FC544A, peach #FFA75D, cream #FFECD4). Reference index grouped discovery/status/ settings/download/reading/diagnostics. .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml: ubuntu/macOS/windows matrix zeitR/mrpheus, plus two axR-specific steps reference workflow doesn’t need: installing libudev-dev Linux runner (something setup-r-dependencies auto-detects prose SystemRequirements field), chmod +x configure cleanup checking (POSIX – Windows uses static Makevars.win). .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml: build-site / coverage-badge / deploy--gh-pages structure zeitR/mrpheus (via JamesIves/github-pages-deploy-action), libudev-dev/ chmod +x steps added reason check workflow. Deviates reference workflow one place: copies logo.png docs/, card.png – axR doesn’t card.png (wider social-preview composite, distinct hex logo ) yet. Add cp line back one exists. man/figures/logo.png (1080x1241, rasterized logo.svg via rsvg-convert) pkgdown/favicon/ (via pkgdown::build_favicons(), hitting realfavicongenerator.net’s API) added – needed pkgdown workflow’s OpenGraph image copy favicon <link> tags respectively; didn’t exist . DESCRIPTION: added covr/pkgdown Suggests; URL now lists pkgdown site alongside GitHub repo, matching zeitR/mrpheus’s convention. README.md: added R CMD CHECK, coverage, pkgdown-site badges, matching zeitR/mrpheus. Netlify deployment (watching gh-pages, domain axr.circadia-lab.uk) manual step outside repo – something commit can configure.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: axivity_discover() yet finding real AX3 device (macOS 26.2), despite ioreg confirming device enumerates correctly IOKit level expected VID/PID (0x04D8/0x0057) serial (CWA17_46171). Root cause yet identified – waiting input Axivity/OMAPI contact. device’s USB mass-storage volume mounts fine independent , axivity_copy_data() () works around meantime. axivity_get_metadata()’s padding-trim regex hasn’t checked real device’s returned buffer.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: Added Mario Leocadio-Miguel author (DESCRIPTION, _pkgdown.yml, README.md, LICENSE, LICENSE.md).

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: Three exit(1) calls udev_new() failure (GetSerialDevice(), InitDeviceFinder(), background OmDeviceDiscoveryThread()) terminate whole R process. Replaced early returns (return; / return NULL; matching function’s signature) – callers already handle failure gracefully (e.g. checking strlen(serial_device) > 0). file never used OmLog() , raw printf()/ fprintf(stderr, ...), including one unconditional debug printf("DEVICE-ACTION: ...") inside background monitoring loop spammed stdout every udev event. converted OmLog() calls (matching Mac finder’s convention) rather just deleted, Linux keeps diagnostic capability via axivity_enable_debug_log(). Untested real hardware Linux – correctness fixes R CMD check flagged, claim Linux device discovery verified working end--end.

what: Vendored code patches (Windows) – unverified, iterating via CI
title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: GUID_DEVINTERFACE_DISK/GUID_DEVINTERFACE_VOLUME undefined link time: fixed defining INITGUID <windows.h> everything pulls , GUID data compiles locally instead needing import library – standard, compiler-agnostic convention (MSVC-specific), low risk. VariantClear/SysFreeString (oleaut32) CLSID_WbemLocator/IID_IWbemLocator (wbemuuid, needed file’s WMI serial-port queries) undefined: added libs Makevars.win’s PKG_LIBS. Confident exist MinGW-w64 Windows SDK port. _com_util::ConvertStringToBSTR (used throughout via _bstr_t/ bstr_t("...") file’s WMI queries) undefined: added -lcomsuppw PKG_LIBS first attempt. Genuinely uncertain resolves – comsuppw historically MSVC-specific library backing _bstr_t, whether Rtools45’s MinGW-w64 ships equivalent isn’t something either us verify. next CI run still fails specifically symbol, real fix rewriting WMI query construction avoid _bstr_t entirely (e.g. via plain SysAllocString()), another library name try.

title: axR 0.0.0.9000
text: src/omapi/omapi-devicefinder-mac.c: kIOMasterPortDefault -> kIOMainPortDefault (2 occurrences), silencing macOS 12+ deprecation warning R CMD check. Purely rename – kIOMasterPortDefault remains functional, isn’t behaviour change. Flagged inline // axR patch comments; re-apply file ever re-vendored upstream libomapi. sprintf() -> snprintf() throughout omapi-status.c, omapi-settings.c, omapi-devicefinder-mac.c (already-fixed-size buffers – adds bound, doesn’t change behaviour). Fixes R CMD check “compiled code” warning calling unbounded [v]sprintf. omapi-main.c: OmStartup()’s default log stream changed stderr NULL (logging unless caller opts via OmSetLogStream()/OmSetLogCallback()). OmLog() already guards om.log != NULL writing, safe – compiled code writing directly stderr instead R’s console exactly check warns , default debug level nothing logged either way. omapi-devicefinder-mac.c: removed custom SIGINT handler (SignalHandler()) called exit(0) directly, plus registration via signal(). vendored library installing process-wide signal handler terminates process actively unsafe embedded host application (R) interrupt handling – real robustness fix, just lint fix. Also removed several fprintf(stderr, ...) calls duplicated adjacent OmLog() call (already supports configurable log streams/callbacks). omapi-devicefinder-mac.c, DeviceNotification(): guard CFRelease(deviceData->deviceName) NULL. crashing R (rsession) device removal *** CFRelease() called NULL *** / EXC_BREAKPOINT current macOS – older CoreFoundation treated CFRelease(NULL) silent -op; recent macOS hardens hard abort. decade-old latent bug vendored code, newly fatal rather newly introduced. Found via real device test (macOS 26.2, AX3) – see crash report’s stack trace (DeviceNotification omapi-devicefinder-mac.c:386) original diagnosis. omapi-devicefinder-mac.c, DeviceNotification(): also stopped calling Release() deviceData->deviceInterface removal – crashed SIGSEGV/EXC_BAD_ACCESS even deviceInterface non-NULL, .e. vtable pointed longer valid time fires (physical device already gone – kIOMessageServiceIsTerminated). Unlike CFRelease(NULL) fix , one inferred crash rather confirmed documented macOS behaviour – flagged provisional pending real-device testing. omapi-devicefinder-mac.c: added idempotency guard (DeviceData.removed) DeviceNotification(), third crash appeared device removal – double-free (free(deviceData) twice, ___BUG_IN_CLIENT_OF_LIBMALLOC_POINTER_BEING_FREED_WAS_NOT_ALLOCATED, SIGABRT), consistent IOKit delivering one kIOMessageServiceIsTerminated single physical unplug current macOS. First attempt guard flawed: stored removed flag inside deviceData, function also free()d deviceData – duplicate call’s guard check reading already-freed memory, undefined behaviour () still let double-free . Fixed longer calling free(deviceData) handler : guard can reliable memory checks stays valid, bounded, negligible leak (one small struct per physical removal event, life R session) accepted exchange guard actually working. Four real crashes found fixed via one afternoon live-device testing (macOS 26.2, AX3). Build system: src/Makevars longer committed static file. configure (POSIX shell script) now generates src/Makevars.install time, picking right device-finder object libraries platform. moves Darwin-vs-Linux conditional Make syntax (ifeq, $(shell), :=) shell syntax, fixing R CMD check “GNU extensions Makefiles” warning. configure must executable (chmod +x configure) – git doesn’t reliably preserve bit across clone/checkout paths, double-check cloning. src/Makevars.win untouched (never conditional logic). cleanup (also POSIX shell script, also needs chmod +x): removes configure-generated src/Makevars leftover .o/./.dll build artefacts (including nested ones src/omapi/, pkgbuild::clean_dll() doesn’t reach). Run R CMD check ’ve built locally beforehand – standard companion configure script per ‘Configure cleanup’ R Extensions manual.
